title: Mapping buildings by construction year (BAG)
text: use administrative boundaries select Stadsdriehoek, historic center Rotterdam.
code: gemeenten <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "gemeente_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2025 ) rotterdam <- filter(gemeenten, statnaam == "Rotterdam")  buurten <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "buurt_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2025,   filter_by = rotterdam, predicate = "within" ) stadsdriehoek <- filter(buurten, statnaam == "Stadsdriehoek")

title: Mapping buildings by construction year (BAG)
text: kadaster/bag dataset pand (building) layer. filter neighborhood; pdok_read() pre-filters server clips exact shape. BAG records unknown construction year 9999; set NA.
code: buildings <- pdok_read("kadaster/bag", "pand", filter_by = stadsdriehoek) #> ⠙ Downloading PDOK features: 363 fetched #> ⠹ Downloading PDOK features: 808 fetched #> ⠹ Downloading PDOK features: 1022 fetched nrow(buildings) #> [1] 1022 buildings <- mutate(buildings, bouwjaar = na_if(bouwjaar, 9999)) range(buildings$bouwjaar, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 1609 2026

title: Mapping buildings by construction year (BAG)
text: Rotterdam’s historic center largely destroyed May 1940 bombing rebuilt afterwards. make dividing line jump , color buildings pre-date bombing (1940) cool blues post-war rebuild warm tones — surviving pre-war buildings stand sea reconstruction.  map interactively — zoom click building:
code: tmap_mode("plot") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()`  era_scale <- tm_scale_intervals(   breaks = c(1600, 1900, 1940, 1960, 1980, 2000, 2026),   values = c("#1f78b4", "#a6cee3", "#ffffb2", "#fecc5c", "#fd8d3c", "#e31a1c"),   # Years are not thousands: drop the "." group separator (1.900 -> 1900).   label.format = tm_label_format(big.mark = "") )  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(buildings) +   tm_polygons(fill = "bouwjaar", fill.scale = era_scale, col = NULL) +   tm_title("Buildings in Rotterdam's Stadsdriehoek, by construction year") +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster") #> [plot mode] fit legend/component: Some legend items or map compoments do not #> fit well, and are therefore rescaled. #> ℹ Set the tmap option `component.autoscale = FALSE` to disable rescaling. tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view"  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(buildings) +   tm_polygons(fill = "bouwjaar", fill.scale = era_scale, col = NULL,               id = "identificatie",               popup = tm_popup(vars = c("Built" = "bouwjaar"),                                format = tm_label_format(big.mark = ""))) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: PDOK basemaps
text: pdok_basemap() returns one two things, set format: raster (default) — classic tile URL ({z}/{x}/{y}, PNG/JPEG). Works tmap, leaflet maplibre/mapgl. safe, universal choice. vector — Mapbox-GL style URL. Works maplibre/mapgl , stays razor-sharp every zoom can tilt 3D. available styles differ per format: (luchtfoto aerial imagery.)
code: pdok_basemap()                                # raster, default style #> [1] "https://service.pdok.nl/brt/achtergrondkaart/wmts/v2_0/standaard/EPSG:3857/{z}/{x}/{y}.png" pdok_basemap("grijs")                         # another raster style #> [1] "https://service.pdok.nl/brt/achtergrondkaart/wmts/v2_0/grijs/EPSG:3857/{z}/{x}/{y}.png" pdok_basemap("standaard", format = "vector")  # a vector style URL #> [1] "https://api.pdok.nl/kadaster/brt-achtergrondkaart/ogc/v1/styles/standaard__webmercatorquad?f=mapbox"

title: PDOK basemaps
text: Hand URL tm_basemap(). twelve province outlines standard PDOK basemap — drawing borders keeps basemap readable: plain leaflet URL urlTemplate: raster basemap ordinary web tiles, also works static maps: switch tmap_mode("plot") (use ggplot2 maptiles/ggspatial) tiles downloaded drawn image. vector style, contrast, rendered live browser, interactive-.
code: provinces <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "provincie_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2025 ) tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()`  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("standaard")) +   tm_shape(provinces) +   tm_borders(col = "#E8631C", lwd = 1.5) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster") leaflet() |>   addTiles(     urlTemplate = pdok_basemap("standaard"),     attribution = "Kaartgegevens © Kadaster"   ) |>   addPolygons(data = st_transform(provinces, 4326)) tmap_mode("plot") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view"  utrecht <- filter(provinces, statnaam == "Utrecht")  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("standaard")) +   tm_shape(utrecht) +   tm_borders(col = "#E8631C", lwd = 2) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: PDOK basemaps
text: five raster styles spot Utrecht. tab just leaflet() |> addTiles(pdok_basemap("<style>")) — style name changes. standaard grijs pastel water luchtfoto

title: PDOK basemaps
text: mapgl brings MapLibre Mapbox GL libraries R: vector maps stay sharp zoom can tilt 3D. GL map takes style URL — exactly pdok_basemap(format = "vector") returns.

title: Thematic maps from CBS statistics
text: quantile classification keeps color class roughly equal size, works well skewed variable like density. Neighborhoods without value (water, parks, industry) drawn gray.  map interactively — hover neighborhood name click value. little transparency lets basemap show , keep bearings: map different figure, swap bevolkingsdichtheid_inwoners_per_km2 another column — aantal_inwoners, stedelijkheid_adressen_per_km2, gemiddelde_woningwaarde, among hundreds others (figures appear year two boundaries).
code: tmap_mode("plot") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()`  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(buurten) +   tm_polygons(     fill = "bevolkingsdichtheid_inwoners_per_km2",     fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(n = 6, style = "quantile",                                     values = "brewer.yl_or_rd"),     fill.legend = tm_legend("Inhabitants per km2"),     col = "white", lwd = 0.2, fill_alpha = 0.8   ) +   tm_title("Population density by neighborhood, Amsterdam (CBS 2025)") +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster") #> [plot mode] fit legend/component: Some legend items or map compoments do not #> fit well, and are therefore rescaled. #> ℹ Set the tmap option `component.autoscale = FALSE` to disable rescaling. tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view"  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(buurten) +   tm_polygons(     fill = "bevolkingsdichtheid_inwoners_per_km2",     fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(n = 6, style = "quantile",                                     values = "brewer.yl_or_rd"),     fill.legend = tm_legend("Inhabitants per km2"),     col = "white", lwd = 0.2, fill_alpha = 0.6,     id = "buurtnaam",     popup = tm_popup(vars = c("Inhabitants per km2" = "bevolkingsdichtheid_inwoners_per_km2"))   ) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: Coordinate reference systems
text: pdok_list_layers() reports, layer, CRSs can deliver (crs) CRS data stored (storage_crs). layer stored RD New (28992) can served several CRSs.
code: layers <- pdok_list_layers("cbs/gebiedsindelingen") gemeente <- layers[layers$layer == "gemeente_gegeneraliseerd", ]  gemeente$crs[[1]] #> [1]  4326 28992  3857  4258 gemeente$storage_crs #> [1] 28992

title: Coordinate reference systems
text: OGC API, PDOK serves GeoJSON, whose default CRS84 (lon/lat). default pdok_read() gives lon/lat: Ask another CRS crs argument. pdokr transformation client-side sf::st_transform(), works target CRS — even one service offer. coordinates differ accordingly — degrees versus meters:
code: gemeenten <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "gemeente_gegeneraliseerd",   datetime = 2025 ) sf::st_crs(gemeenten)$epsg #> [1] 4326 gemeenten_rd <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "gemeente_gegeneraliseerd",   datetime = 2025, crs = 28992 ) sf::st_crs(gemeenten_rd)$epsg #> [1] 28992 sf::st_bbox(gemeenten) #>      xmin      ymin      xmax      ymax  #>  3.358378 50.750367  7.227498 53.554047 sf::st_bbox(gemeenten_rd) #>     xmin     ymin     xmax     ymax  #>  13565.4 306846.2 278026.1 619231.6

title: Coordinate reference systems
text: data plotted lon/lat RD New picture. Lon/lat unprojected, Dutch latitudes (~52° N) country looks horizontally stretched; RD New accurate planar projection. lon/lat graticule (grid meridians parallels) makes difference obvious: perfect rectangular grid lon/lat, lines run slightly skewed RD New, projection gently rotated relative north.   analysis meters (distances, areas, buffers) use RD New; web maps, lon/lat Web Mercator.
code: tmap_mode("plot") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()` tm_shape(gemeenten) +   tm_polygons(fill = "grey95", col = "grey50", lwd = 0.3) +   tm_graticules(col = "steelblue", lwd = 0.5) +   tm_title("Lon/lat (EPSG:4326)") tm_shape(gemeenten_rd) +   tm_polygons(fill = "grey95", col = "grey50", lwd = 0.3) +   tm_graticules(col = "steelblue", lwd = 0.5) +   tm_title("RD New (EPSG:28992)")

what: Two things pdokr handles for you
title: Coordinate reference systems
text: bbox pre-filter always CRS84. Whatever CRS bbox filter_by geometry , pdokr converts extent lon/lat sending server, OGC bbox parameter expects. Axis order. “CRS84” means longitude/latitude order, EPSG:4326 formally latitude/longitude. sf consistently stores coordinates x = longitude, y = latitude, pdokr follows , think axis swaps.

title: Combining with external data: school locations (DUO)
text: onderwijslocaties resource lists every location education may take place, coordinates BAG_ID. catch: possible education locations, recognized schools. conference center hosts odd exam, commercial training firm, . Mapping straight away put dots school exists.
code: locations <- read_duo("a7e3f323-6e46-4dca-a834-369d9d520aa8") nrow(locations) #> [1] 13605

title: Combining with external data: school locations (DUO)
text: find schools recognized law combine two DUO tables. relaties... resource links location (ONDERWIJSLOCATIECODE) recognized institution (VESTIGINGSCODE); vestigingserkenningen resource describes institution — name education law falls (WET). tables historical, row counts EINDDATUM. locations backed recognized institution remain — training venues, exam halls — carries school name education sector. One quirk worth knowing: university college registered single recognized institution, official location appears , every building. map therefore dominated primary secondary schools, register site.
code: relations    <- read_duo("c18ec7dd-aa4e-4b51-997c-782955f1aa38") institutions <- read_duo("01fd2a5f-40af-456f-864d-13265a51e5e2")  # translate the education-law code into a readable sector sectors <- c(WPO = "Primary", WVO = "Secondary", WEC = "Special",              WEB = "Vocational (MBO)", WHW = "Higher education")  # current institutions (no end date), one row each, with a readable sector institutions <- institutions |>   filter(is.na(EINDDATUM)) |>   distinct(VESTIGINGSCODE, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   mutate(sector = unname(sectors[WET])) |>   select(VESTIGINGSCODE, sector, school = VOLLEDIGE_NAAM)  # current location -> recognized institution, one institution per location link <- relations |>   filter(is.na(EINDDATUM)) |>   select(ONDERWIJSLOCATIECODE, VESTIGINGSCODE) |>   inner_join(institutions, by = "VESTIGINGSCODE") |>   distinct(ONDERWIJSLOCATIECODE, .keep_all = TRUE)  # inner join drops every location without a recognized institution schools <- inner_join(locations, link, by = "ONDERWIJSLOCATIECODE") nrow(schools) #> [1] 9675 count(schools, sector) #>             sector    n #> 1 Higher education   62 #> 2          Primary 7229 #> 3        Secondary 1473 #> 4          Special  708 #> 5 Vocational (MBO)  203

what: Make it spatial and combine with PDOK
title: Combining with external data: school locations (DUO)
text: coordinates WGS84 longitude/latitude, build sf object use pdok_filter_by() municipal boundary zoom Utrecht.
code: schools <- schools |>   filter(!is.na(GPS_LONGITUDE), !is.na(GPS_LATITUDE)) |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("GPS_LONGITUDE", "GPS_LATITUDE"), crs = 4326)  gemeenten <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "gemeente_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2025 ) utrecht <- filter(gemeenten, statnaam == "Utrecht")  utrecht_schools <- pdok_filter_by(schools, utrecht, predicate = "within") nrow(utrecht_schools) #> [1] 186 tmap_mode("plot") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()`  tm_shape(utrecht) +   tm_polygons(fill = "grey95", col = "grey60") +   tm_shape(utrecht_schools) +   tm_dots(     fill = "sector", size = 0.5,     fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(values = "brewer.set2"),     fill.legend = tm_legend("Sector")   ) +   tm_title("Recognized schools in Utrecht, by sector")

title: Combining with external data: school locations (DUO)
text: school sits building BAG. read pand (building) layer historic center, keep footprints contain school, carry school name sector across spatial join. result real school buildings, colored sector. Click one school uses .
code: wijken <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "wijk_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2025,   filter_by = utrecht, predicate = "within" ) binnenstad <- filter(wijken, grepl("Binnenstad", statnaam)) centre_schools <- pdok_filter_by(utrecht_schools, binnenstad, predicate = "within")  panden <- pdok_read("kadaster/bag", "pand", filter_by = binnenstad) #> ⠙ Downloading PDOK features: 1058 fetched #> ⠹ Downloading PDOK features: 2555 fetched #> ⠸ Downloading PDOK features: 4479 fetched #> ⠼ Downloading PDOK features: 6026 fetched school_buildings <- panden |>   st_filter(centre_schools) |>   st_join(select(centre_schools, school, sector, STRAATNAAM)) nrow(school_buildings) #> [1] 11 tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view"  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(school_buildings) +   tm_polygons(     fill = "sector",     fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(values = "brewer.set2"),     fill.legend = tm_legend("Sector"),     col = "grey30", lwd = 0.5, id = "school",     popup = tm_popup(vars = c("School" = "school", "Sector" = "sector",                               "Street" = "STRAATNAAM"))   ) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: Filtering data by area
text: Pass filter geometry pdok_read() filter_by. uses polygon’s bounding box cheap server-side pre-filter, clips result exact shape. keep national parks lie within Fryslân. static map province national parks:  interactive mode — zoom click park:
code: parks <- pdok_read(   "rvo/nationale-parken-geharmoniseerd", "protectedsite",   filter_by = fryslan ) parks$text #> [1] "Lauwersmeer"        "Schiermonnikoog"    "De Alde Feanen"     #> [4] "Drents-Friese Wold" tmap_mode("plot") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()`  tm_shape(fryslan) +   tm_borders(col = "grey40") +   tm_shape(parks) +   tm_polygons(fill = "text", fill.legend = tm_legend("National park")) +   tm_title("National parks in Fryslân") tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view"  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(parks) +   tm_polygons(fill = "text", fill_alpha = 0.6, id = "text",               fill.legend = tm_legend("National park")) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: Filtering data by area
text: filter_by folds two-stage workflow one call. can also run two stages , useful want reuse pre-filtered data apply different spatial predicate. pdok_filter_by() reconciles coordinate reference systems accepts predicate sets feature must relate area kept: "intersects" (default — touching area way), "within" (lying entirely inside ), "contains", "disjoint" (everything outside), specialized "overlaps", "touches", "crosses", "covers" "covered_by". maps matching sf function sf::st_<predicate>(); see ?sf::geos_binary_pred precise definition . plain-sf equivalent, objects share CRS, :
code: # Stage 1: cheap server-side pre-filter by the bounding box of Fryslân parks_in_bbox <- pdok_read(   "rvo/nationale-parken-geharmoniseerd", "protectedsite",   bbox = fryslan )  # Stage 2: exact client-side clip to the polygon pdok_filter_by(parks_in_bbox, fryslan)$text #> [1] "Lauwersmeer"        "Schiermonnikoog"    "De Alde Feanen"     #> [4] "Drents-Friese Wold" fryslan_ll <- sf::st_transform(fryslan, sf::st_crs(parks_in_bbox)) parks_in_bbox[fryslan_ll, , op = sf::st_intersects]$text #> [1] "Lauwersmeer"        "Schiermonnikoog"    "De Alde Feanen"     #> [4] "Drents-Friese Wold"

title: Geocoding: from names to locations
text: simplest form pass query get back single best match. ordinary sf object point geometry, ready map.
code: home <- pdok_geocode("Domplein 1, Utrecht") home |> select(weergavenaam, type, score) #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 3 fields #> Geometry type: MULTILINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 5.12111 ymin: 52.09004 xmax: 5.1224 ymax: 52.09118 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   weergavenaam      type  score                                         geometry #>   <chr>             <chr> <dbl>                            <MULTILINESTRING [°]> #> 1 Domplein, Utrecht weg    15.4 ((5.12193 52.09004, 5.12149 52.09093), (5.12149… tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view" #> ℹ toggle with `tmap::ttm()`  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(home) +   tm_dots(fill = "#E8631C", size = 1) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: Geocoding: from names to locations
text: Locatieserver know addresses. Every result type, geometry get back depends — point pinpoint locations, line road, polygon area. default pdok_geocode() returns best match type, ranked service’s relevance score. convenient, single name often exists several levels — “Utrecht” municipality, province, town: Pass type pin exactly level mean. query returns different geometry: Seen together, three levels nest neatly — address sits road, lies inside district.
code: pdok_geocode("Utrecht", limit = 5) |>   select(weergavenaam, type, score) #> Simple feature collection with 5 features and 3 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 4.9701 ymin: 52.02628 xmax: 5.19515 ymax: 52.14205 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   weergavenaam                  type       score                        geometry #>   <chr>                         <chr>      <dbl>              <MULTIPOLYGON [°]> #> 1 Gemeente Utrecht              gemeente    9.78 (((5.01801 52.06222, 5.01707 5… #> 2 Utrecht, Utrecht, Utrecht     woonplaats  8.82 (((5.01514 52.11395, 5.01553 5… #> 3 Haarzuilens, Utrecht, Utrecht woonplaats  8.59 (((4.97775 52.13057, 4.97394 5… #> 4 Vleuten, Utrecht, Utrecht     woonplaats  8.59 (((4.98085 52.10015, 4.98933 5… #> 5 De Meern, Utrecht, Utrecht    woonplaats  8.52 (((5.0429 52.08946, 5.04283 52… point <- pdok_geocode("Domplein 1, Utrecht")          # adres  -> point road  <- pdok_geocode("Oudegracht, Utrecht", type = "weg")   # weg    -> line area  <- pdok_geocode("Binnenstad, Utrecht", type = "wijk")  # wijk   -> polygon  sf::st_geometry_type(point) #> [1] MULTILINESTRING #> 18 Levels: GEOMETRY POINT LINESTRING POLYGON MULTIPOINT ... TRIANGLE sf::st_geometry_type(road) #> [1] MULTILINESTRING #> 18 Levels: GEOMETRY POINT LINESTRING POLYGON MULTIPOINT ... TRIANGLE sf::st_geometry_type(area) #> [1] MULTIPOLYGON #> 18 Levels: GEOMETRY POINT LINESTRING POLYGON MULTIPOINT ... TRIANGLE tm_shape(area) +   tm_polygons(fill = "#F7F3EC", col = "grey50") +   tm_shape(road) +   tm_lines(col = "#1f78b4", lwd = 2) +   tm_shape(point) +   tm_dots(fill = "#E8631C", size = 0.6) +   tm_title("Three search levels: district, road, address")

title: Geocoding: from names to locations
text: geocoding earns place workflow. Many tasks start “data inside area” — pdok_geocode() gives area boundary without find load administrative layer first. geocoded gemeente, wijk provincie polygon drops straight filter_by. geocoded boundary filtering; result every neighborhood inside municipality De Bilt. interactive map can see hover neighborhood names: pattern works layer: geocode area care , read buildings, parcels statistics within .
code: boundary <- pdok_geocode("De Bilt", type = "gemeente")  # read the neighborhoods that meet the boundary, then keep those whose center # lies inside it. A geocoded boundary is generalized, so it clips edge # neighborhoods unevenly; `predicate = "within"` alone would drop some, while # `"intersects"` pulls in slivers of neighboring municipalities. buurten <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "buurt_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2025,   filter_by = boundary, predicate = "intersects" ) centers <- suppressWarnings(sf::st_centroid(buurten)) buurten <- filter(buurten, lengths(sf::st_within(centers, boundary)) > 0) nrow(buurten) #> [1] 24 tmap_mode("view") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" - "view"  tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(buurten) +   tm_polygons(     fill = "statnaam",     fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(values = "brewer.set3"),     fill.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),     col = "white", fill_alpha = 0.6, id = "statnaam"   ) +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: Geocoding: from names to locations
text: pdok_geocode() takes vector queries, can geocode column addresses one call. Every result row carries query column identifying input came , keeps output aligned input — even query returns several candidates, none. Free search ranks matches across levels — exact address, also street place sits — returns best-ranked one per query. keep eye type column: , two queries resolve street (weg) rather house number, geometry line, point. Pin level type know want. type = "adres" returns exact addresses, points:

title: Geocoding: from names to locations
text: Sometimes coordinates — GPS points, sensor locations, spot map — want know . pdok_reverse_geocode() takes sf points returns nearest address, road place, afstand column giving distance meters. point_id column maps match back input point. default returns nearest match type — usually address. Use type choose level: type = "weg" nearest road, type = "gemeente" municipality point falls (distance 0, since point inside ). input can CRS — RD New points work just well; converted lon/lat query automatically.

text: Pick area administrative boundaries, load another layer within . take historic center Delft map every building .  articles package website walk workflows, interactive, zoomable maps.
code: library(pdokr) library(tmap)  # A municipality, and one of its districts (the historic center of Delft) gemeenten <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "gemeente_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2024 ) delft <- gemeenten[gemeenten$statnaam == "Delft", ]  wijken <- pdok_read(   "cbs/gebiedsindelingen", "wijk_gegeneraliseerd", datetime = 2024,   filter_by = delft, predicate = "within" ) binnenstad <- wijken[wijken$statnaam == "Wijk 11 Binnenstad", ]  # Every building in that district, from the TOP10NL topography buildings <- pdok_read("brt/top10nl", "gebouw_vlak", filter_by = binnenstad)  # Map it tmap_mode("plot") tm_basemap(pdok_basemap("grijs")) +   tm_shape(binnenstad) +   tm_borders(col = "grey40") +   tm_shape(buildings) +   tm_polygons(fill = "#d95f02", col = "white", lwd = 0.4) +   tm_title("Buildings in the historic center of Delft") +   tm_credits("Kaartgegevens © Kadaster")

title: Spatially filter an sf layer by any polygon — pdok_filter_by
text: data sf object filter (example layer loaded pdok_read()). filter_geometry sf sfc object whose geometry defines area interest. predicate spatial relationship test, one "intersects", "within", "contains", "overlaps", "touches", "crosses", "covers", "covered_by", "disjoint". See Spatial predicates section , sf::geos_binary_pred full definitions.

title: Spatially filter an sf layer by any polygon — pdok_filter_by
text: sf object: subset data whose features satisfy predicate respect filter_geometry. zero-row sf returned (silently) feature matches.

title: Spatially filter an sf layer by any polygon — pdok_filter_by
text: filter_geometry can polygon: municipality pdok_read() CBS administrative boundaries, nature reserve, water-authority area, hand-drawn polygon, another PDOK layer. plain-sf equivalent data[filter_geometry, , op = sf::st_intersects] (matching CRS); use prefer drop sf.

title: Spatially filter an sf layer by any polygon — pdok_filter_by
text: predicate selects feature must relate filter_geometry kept. value calls matching sf predicate function sf::st_<predicate>() ("within" uses sf::st_within(), "intersects" uses sf::st_intersects(), ). common choices: "intersects" (default) — feature touches filter_geometry way (overlaps, inside, shares boundary). permissive; usual choice "everything area". "within" — feature lies entirely inside filter_geometry. Use exclude features stick partly area. "contains" — feature entirely encloses filter_geometry (inverse "within"). "disjoint" — feature touch filter_geometry (everything outside area). "overlaps", "touches", "crosses", "covers" "covered_by" remaining, specialized DE-9IM relationships. precise definition , see sf help page sf::geos_binary_pred (topic documents st_intersects(), st_within(), rest).

title: Geocode addresses or place names with the PDOK Locatieserver — pdok_geocode
text: query character vector one non-empty search strings, e.g. "Park Arenberg 88, De Bilt". type Optional result type restrict , one "adres", "postcode", "weg", "woonplaats", "gemeente", "provincie", "buurt", "wijk", "perceel", "hectometerpaal", "appartementsrecht". NULL (default) returns best matches type, ranked service's relevance score. Use type disambiguate names exist several categories (example "Utrecht" municipality province). crs Optional output CRS EPSG code (e.g. 28992). NULL keeps source CRS (CRS84, lon/lat). limit Maximum number results return per query (default 1).

title: Read a PDOK layer as an sf object — pdok_read

title: Read a PDOK layer as an sf object — pdok_read
code: pdok_read(   dataset,   layer,   bbox = NULL,   filter_by = NULL,   predicate = "intersects",   datetime = NULL,   crs = NULL,   max_features = NULL )

title: Read a PDOK layer as an sf object — pdok_read
text: dataset dataset id pdok_list_datasets() (e.g. "cbs/gebiedsindelingen"), raw OGC API base URL. layer layer id pdok_list_layers(). bbox Optional server-side bounding-box pre-filter: numeric vector c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) (assumed CRS84) sf/sfc/bbox object whose extent used. filter_by Optional sf/sfc geometry filter result . bounding box used cheap server-side pre-filter, result filtered exactly pdok_filter_by(). one-call form load--filter workflow. usually polygon (e.g. municipality), point works : filtering area layer point returns feature contains (example municipality address falls ). predicate Spatial predicate filter_by, passed pdok_filter_by() (default "intersects"). See Spatial predicates section available options, sf::geos_binary_pred definitions. datetime Optional temporal filter: single year (e.g. 2026, mapped mid-year instant), OGC datetime string, interval "2020-01-01/2025-12-31". crs Optional output CRS EPSG code (e.g. 28992 RD New). NULL keeps source CRS. max_features Optional cap number features returned.

title: Read a PDOK layer as an sf object — pdok_read
text: sf object one row per feature, layer's attribute columns, geometry column. zero-row sf returned (warning) nothing matches.

title: Read a PDOK layer as an sf object — pdok_read
text: default data returned coordinate reference system service provides (lon/lat, CRS84, OGC path). Set crs receive data another CRS; transformation done client-side sf::st_transform().

what: pdokr: Access Open Geodata from the Dutch 'PDOK' Platform — pdokr-package
title: pdokr: Access Open Geodata from the Dutch 'PDOK' Platform — pdokr-package
text: Tools discover, download, spatially filter open geographic data 'PDOK' (Publieke Dienstverlening Op de Kaart), national geodata platform Netherlands. Datasets layers searched loaded vector simple feature ('sf') objects 'OGC' API Features endpoints, automatic pagination explicit coordinate reference system handling. Loaded layers can filtered polygon area, addresses place names can geocoded 'PDOK' location server. focus vector feature data; raster, tile, coverage services scope. See https://www.pdok.nl/ information platform services.

title: pdokr: Access Open Geodata from the Dutch 'PDOK' Platform — pdokr-package
text: pdokr client PDOK's OGC API Features services. reads vector feature data — points, lines, polygons — returns sf objects. Raster, tile, coverage services (elevation grids map-tile backgrounds) scope: pdok_read() loads features . handful datasets listed pdok_list_datasets() serve tiles coverages rather features; read sf, pdok_read() reports clearly. official PDOK map background, use pdok_basemap().

title: pdokr: Access Open Geodata from the Dutch 'PDOK' Platform — pdokr-package
text: Maintainer: Coen Eisma coeneisma@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Coen Eisma coeneisma@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

title: pdokr 0.1.0
text: Dataset layer discovery pdok_list_datasets(), pdok_search_datasets(), pdok_list_layers() pdok_search_layers(), fetched live PDOK index. Loading layers sf objects pdok_read(), automatic pagination, server-side bounding-box temporal pre-filtering, client-side CRS transformation. pdokr OGC API Features client reads vector features ; dataset serves tiles coverages instead reported clear error. Spatial filtering polygon (point) pdok_filter_by(), also available one call filter_by argument pdok_read(). Geocoding addresses place names pdok_geocode(), returning sf points administrative polygons. query accepts vector, many locations can geocoded one call; query column maps result row back input. Reverse geocoding pdok_reverse_geocode(): sf points nearest address, road place, distance meters. official PDOK basemap map background pdok_basemap(), returning raster tile URL (tmap/leaflet) vector style URL (maplibre/mapgl).
